examples.text: أَیَحۡسَبُ ٱلۡإِنسَـٰنُ أَلَّن نَّجۡمَعَ عِظَامَهُۥ (٣) بَلَىٰ قَـٰدِرِینَ عَلَىٰۤ أَن نُّسَوِّیَ بَنَانَهُۥ (٤)
examples.english: 3. Thinketh man that We shall not assemble his bones? 4. Yea, verily. We are Able to restore his very fingers!

senses.gloss: used in the construct state in ways which are translated into Standard Average European languages as prepositions like except, but, short of; of very frequent occurrence (followed by genitive)

examples.english: I love no one but that woman.
Who will save the purity of the Iraq if not us?
